title: Defense Innovation Unit at AUVSI Xponential 2024
description: We're a fast-moving DoD organization contracting with commercial companies to solve national security problems
text:
Defense Innovation Unit team members will be in San Diego for the 2024 AUVSI Xponential conference. We are excited to share our knowledge and experiences around autonomous systems and the defense innovation base. DIU panelists will touch on topics including the DIU 3.0 strategy, our Blue UAS platforms, commercial tech integration within DoD, and more.
We also encourage you to drop us your contact information and stay connected with DIU news and solicitations. We hope to see you in San Diego.
Tuesday, April 23
Panel: Day 1 Keynote
Details: 9:00 a.m. - 9:45 a.m., Ballroom 20
Description: DIU Director Doug Beck will discuss operationalizing the DIU 3.0 strategy.
Speakers:
Courtney Albon, Space and Emerging Technology Reporter, Defense News
Doug Beck, Director, Defense Innovation Unit
Panel: Innovative Business Models for Enhanced DoD Agility: A Non Technology Approach
Details: 11:00 a.m. - 12:30 p.m., Room 31 B/C
Description: This workshop will challenge the status quo of technology-centric innovation. In this session, industry leaders will share successful business models that go beyond technology, focusing on how to optimize processes, workflows, and strategies to enhance Department of Defense (DoD) agility.
Speakers:
David Michelson, Autonomy Portfolio Director, Defense Innovation Unit
Nathan Parker, General Manager, Defense and Public Sector, Kodiak Robotics
John Mark Wilson, Head of Strategy and Communications, Applied Intuition Defense
Rachel Braun, Partner, Space Ventures, AFWERX and SpaceWERX
Panel: Human Machine Integration
Details: 11:15 a.m. - 12 p.m., Defense Theater
Description: The acceleration of uncrewed systems in military and commercial operations can no longer be seen as simply human-to-machine. The panel will address the nascent point of human-machine integration (HMI) and discuss the opportunities and challenges ahead.
Speakers:
John Burke, Specialist Executive, Deloitte (Moderator)
Christopher Orlowski, Associated Director and Growth Lead, Land Systems, Anduril Industries
Kevin O’Brien, Phd, Technical Director, Autonomy Portfolio, Defense Innovation Unit
Ryan Fitzgerald, Autonomy Lead, DON Office of Strategic Assessment
Stuart Hatfield, Army G-8 HMI Director, U.S. Army
Panel: The Blue List, Framework, NDAA Compliance and How It Ties Together.
Details: 1:00 p.m. - 1:25 p.m., Innovation Theather
Description: Defense Innovation Unit’s Trent Emeneker will provide an overview of the past, present, and projected future of the DIU Blue List and Blue Framework List, how NDAA compliance affects folks, what the goods and bads are from the perspective of industry as voiced to us, DoD voices, and how DIU is trying to partner and solve problems in this space.
Speaker:
Trent Emeneker, Autonomy Portfolio, Defense Innovation Unit
Panel: Additive Manufacturing and Advanced Materials Use Cases for Attributable Unmanned Systems
Details: 1:50 p.m. - 2:30 p.m., Defense Theater
Description: This panel will explore how additive manufacturing and advanced materials innovations can impact the U.S. Defense industrial base and be utilized in attritable unmanned systems defense initiatives.
Speakers:
Alex Mercer, Senior Manager Content & Product Strategy, American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (Moderator)
Alex Angilella, Lead Aeromechanical Engineer, the MITRE Corporation
Andy Chapman, Entrepreneur, Skip Dynamix Corporation
Jeff Ratcliff, Business Development Executive, RapidFlight
Michael Kenworthy, Chief Technology Officer, Divergent Technologies, Inc.
Travis DeMeester, Program Manager, Defense Innovation Unit
Panel: Counter Unmanned Aircraft Systems
Details: 3:30 p.m. to 4 p.m., Defense Theater
Description: This will be a multi-domain CUAS discussion on the layered CUAS defense strategy employed across the joint force and opportunities for where commercial innovation can offer support.
Speakers:
Joshua Zike, Program Manager, Defense Innovation Unit (Moderator)
Leo McCloskey, VP Marketing, Echodyne
Matt McCrann, CEO, DroneSheild
Panel: Assumed Breach: Cybersecurity, Compliance and Risk Assessment
Details: 4:30 p.m. - 5 p.m., room 28 D/E
Description: These two speakers will dive into the critical realm of cybersecurity risks in the dynamic landscape of drones, supply chain data, and government customer engagements. They will explore concise insights, practical strategies, and real-world constraints as industry leaders and experts share their experiences in safeguarding uncrewed systems against evolving cyber threats.
Speakers:
